meta.siteDescription: Full Stack Developer, Software Architect, and DevOps. Technical portfolio with projects, architectures, and experience.

Hero.role: Full Stack Developer · Software Architect · DevOps
Hero.tagline: I lead end-to-end products and platforms: scalable system design, full-stack delivery, and cloud operations.
Hero.ctaCvSoonTitle: CV download is coming soon — stay tuned.

About.title: About
About.p1: Six-plus years building web applications and leading initiatives where frontend, backend, architecture, and DevOps meet. I have worked as a software architect in consulting, led teams in fintech, and currently combine senior development with university teaching and entrepreneurship (Kahani E.A.S.).
About.p2: I focus on maintainable solutions tied to the business: clear module boundaries, explicit API contracts, observability, and repeatable deployments. I enjoy turning ambiguous requirements into designs teams can ship with confidence.
About.p3: I can lead whole projects: from context diagrams and architectural decisions to CI/CD pipelines, container infrastructure, and polished user experiences.

Ai.title: Artificial intelligence
Ai.subtitle: How I integrate AI across development and product work—with engineering discipline and responsibility.
Ai.p1: I use language models and assistant tooling as accelerators—not as a substitute for design or human review: draft code, API exploration, documentation, and test-assisted workflows.
Ai.p2: I focus on pairing solid engineering practice with flows where AI improves repeatability and clarity: versioned prompts, context boundaries, output validation, and traceability when sensitive data is involved.
highlights: API design assistance, refactors, and LLM-assisted reviews with mandatory human sign-off.
Practical integrations (embeddings, lightweight RAG) when the domain warrants it and sources are trustworthy.
Trade-offs on cost, latency, and privacy: what to delegate to the model vs. rules and automated tests.
Continuous learning: tracking ecosystem changes without replacing architecture fundamentals.
